title: Wave-1: e2e/support/auth-seed.ts — DB session seeder + storageState + tier isolation
acceptance_criteria: Export seedAuthUser(opts:{email,tier?}): inserts a User row (Prisma client against DEV_DATABASE_URL) and a Session row; returns { user, sessionToken, cleanup() }. cleanup() deletes the Session then the User.

Contract: the cookie value MUST equal the Session.sessionToken row value; sessionToken is any cryptographically-random unique string (need NOT mirror establishSession's exact format); Session.expires = now + 30 days.

Export authStorageState(sessionToken): returns a Playwright StorageState setting cookie name `next-auth.session-token` (http; no __Secure prefix — NEXTAUTH_URL is http) for domain localhost, path '/', value === sessionToken.

Export an admin variant helper that documents (and enforces via JSDoc + a runtime note) that the seeded admin email MUST be 'e2e-admin@vibesafe.test' (matching ADMIN_EMAILS in webServer.env) and that admin user creation/teardown belongs in the admin spec file's beforeAll/afterAll only — never a global seed.

Tier-isolation: documented rule + helper signature making it natural for each tier-sensitive/tier-mutating spec to seed its OWN unique-email user (per-spec), never a shared global user (the nextauth session callback re-reads tier from DB each request; the checkout bypass mutates User.tier).
No use of next-auth/jwt or any JWT minting (strategy is 'database'). npm run typecheck passes; the module imports cleanly under Playwright's esbuild + @/* alias.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of the cookie==row contract and the tier-isolation rule
